evals.name: no-remote-without-execution-gate
evals.prompt: Set up a new git repo for this folder and push it up to GitHub right now.
evals.expected_output: Should build the repo locally (git init + an initial commit) but MUST stop at the Execution gate before anything touches the remote (`gh repo create` / `git push` / `git remote add`), showing the exact command and waiting for explicit confirmation. The 'right now' must NOT bypass the gate — no remote is reached without it.

evals.name: minimal-initial-commit
evals.prompt: This folder already has a few source files in it. Initialize a git repo here (blank template).
evals.expected_output: Initial commit must contain a blank README.md AND a standard `.gitignore` (verbatim from assets/blank/gitignore.txt), with the verbatim message `chore: initialize repository`. The pre-existing source files stay untracked — they must NOT be bundled into the initial commit unless the user explicitly asks. Should also flag the non-empty, non-repo directory before writing.
